modules.name: yices.InterpolationContext
documentation:
The InterpolationContext class mimics, but doesn't wrap the yices interpolation_context_t struct.

It provides access to the yices_check_context_with_interpolation api call in a pythonesque manner.

modules.name: yices.Context
documentation:
Contexts wrap the important yices2 context_t structure.

A context contains one or more solvers and supports operations for
manipulating assertions and for checking whether these assertions are
satisfiable. If they are, a model can be constructed from the context.

methods.name: yices.Context.Context.enable_option
documentation: option is a string

methods.name: yices.Context.Context.disable_option
documentation: option is a string

methods.name: yices.Context.Context.assert_formulas
documentation: to be very pythonesque we should handle iterables, but we do need to know the length

methods.name: yices.Context.Context.population
documentation: returns the current live population of Context objects.

modules.name: yices.Dimacs
documentation:
Yices allows one to bit blast and then export the results out to a file in the DIMACS format.

This class encapsulates this feature.

methods.name: yices.Dimacs.Dimacs.export_formula
documentation:
Bit-blast then export the CNF to a file, returns a pair consisting of a boolean
and a status. The boolean is True if the file was written, or False indicating
that the status of the formulas was determined, and is the second component.

If the simplify flag is true, then is also possible for CNF
simplification to detect that the CNF is sat or unsat.
new in 2.6.2

methods.name: yices.Dimacs.Dimacs.export_formulas
documentation:
Bit-blast then export the CNFs to a file, returns a pair consisting of a boolean
and a status. The boolean is True if the file was written, or False indicating
that the status of the formulas was determined, and is the second component.

If the simplify flag is true, then is also possible for CNF
simplification to detect that the CNF is sat or unsat.
new in 2.6.2

modules.name: yices.Parameters
documentation:
The Parameters class wraps the yicesparameter record structure param_t .

A parameter record stores search parameters and options that control the heuristics used by a solver.

documentation: returns the current live population of Parameters objects.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.has_delegate
documentation:
Returns True if the underlying libyices has been compiled with the given delegate supported.

Valid delegates are "cadical", "cryptominisat", and "y2sat".
new in 2.6.2

documentation: Must be called before any other API routine (other than is_inited), to initialize internal data structures.

documentation: Deletes all the internal data structure, must be called on exiting to prevent leaks.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.reset
documentation: Resets all the internal data structures.

documentation: Returns a newly allocated context; a context is a stack of assertions.
CONTEXTS    #

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.free_context
documentation: Frees the given context.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.context_status
documentation: The context status.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.reset_context
documentation: Removes all assertions from the context.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.push
documentation: Marks a backtrack point in the context.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.pop
documentation: Backtracks to the previous backtrack point.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.context_enable_option
documentation: Used to tune the amount of simplification used when evaluating assertions.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.context_disable_option
documentation: Used to tune the amount of simplification used when evaluating assertions.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.assert_formula
documentation: Assert the formula t in the context ctx.

documentation: Assert an array of formulas of length n in the context ctx.

documentation: Checks whether all the assertions stored in the context ctx are satisfiable.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.check_context_with_assumptions
documentation: Checks whether the assertions in the context ctx together with n assumptions are satisfiable.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.assert_blocking_clause
documentation: Adds a blocking clause, this is intended to help enumerate different models for a set of assertions.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.stop_search
documentation: Interupts the search.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.get_unsat_core
documentation: Compute an unsat core after a call to yices_check_with_assumptions.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.new_model
documentation: Builds a model from scratch.
MODELS     #

documentation: Builds a model from the context ctx.

methods.name: yices.Yices.Yices.free_model
documentation: Frees the model.

modules.name: yices.Delegates
documentation:
Yices has the ability to use third-party SAT solvers as backends to the bit-vector solvers.

The Delegate class encapulates this feature of the API.

methods.name: yices.Delegates.Delegates.has_delegate
documentation:
Returns True if the underlying libyices has been compiled with the given delegate supported.

Valid delegates are "cadical", "cryptominisat", and "y2sat".
new in 2.6.2

methods.name: yices.Delegates.Delegates.check_formula
documentation:
Checks whether the formula f is satisfiable in the logic using the delegate.

If the formula is satisfiable, and the model_array is not None, then it
inserts a model  into the beginning of the array.
new in 2.6.2

methods.name: yices.Delegates.Delegates.check_formulas
documentation:
Checks whether the formulas in the array are satisfiable in the logic using the delegate.

If the formulas are satisfiable, and the model_array is not None, then it
inserts a model into the beginning of the array.
new in 2.6.2

Declares a new scalar type with the given element_names, which

It returns the yices term for the type, and the list of yices terms
corresponding to the element with the associated name.

modules.name: yices.Model
documentation:
The Model class wraps the Yices model_t structure.

If a context is satisfiable, Yices can build a model of the context’s
assertions. Functions are provided to extract the values of terms in a
model. Atomic values (e.g., integer or bitvector constants) can be
obtained directly. Non-atomic values—that is, tuples or functions—are
represented internally as nodes in a DAG. The API includes functions
to explore this DAG and get the values of tuples or functions.

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.get_scalar_value
documentation: Returns the index of the value. This is the low level version, and does not use yapi.yices_constant.

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.set_bool
documentation: set the value of a boolean term.

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.set_integer
documentation: set the value of an integer term.

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.set_fraction
documentation: set the value of an real term.

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.set_bv
documentation: set the value of an bv term.

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.set_bv_from_array
documentation: set the value of an bv term from an array of integers.

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.get_value_from_algebraic_yval
documentation: this problem is part of the gmp libpoly conundrum

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.get_value_as_term
documentation: yices tuples should be returned as python tuples
yices functions should be returned as closures (i.e functions)

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.support_for_term
documentation: Returns the list of uninterpreted terms that fix the value of the given term in the model.
new in 2.6.2
term support

methods.name: yices.Model.Model.support_for_terms
documentation: Returns the list of uninterpreted terms that fix the value in the model of every term in the given array.
new in 2.6.2
term array support

documentation: returns the current live population of Model objects.
